skills.name: native-feel-cross-platform-desktop
skills.description: Architecture and audit guidance for cross-platform desktop apps that must feel native across macOS and Windows, covering native shell boundaries, system WebView usage, typed IPC, memory trade-offs, and native-feel conventions.
recommendedFor: Designing or rewriting cross-platform desktop apps that need a native feel
Evaluating Electron, Tauri, WebView, or native-shell architecture trade-offs
Auditing WebView-based desktop UI polish, launch feel, memory, IPC, and platform conventions
skills.relationshipToFirstPartySkills: Complements skill-recommender as a third-party candidate for desktop architecture and native-feel UI decisions; it is not a replacement for risk-oriented-code-review.

skills.name: ponytail
skills.description: Minimalist engineering guidance that pushes agents toward YAGNI, reuse, standard library and native platform features, smallest correct diffs, and over-engineering review/audit workflows.
recommendedFor: Keeping implementation scope minimal without removing required safety checks
Reviewing diffs for over-engineering, speculative abstractions, redundant dependencies, and avoidable boilerplate
Auditing a repository for code that can be deleted, simplified, or replaced with standard library or native platform features
skills.relationshipToFirstPartySkills: Complements risk-oriented-code-review by focusing on over-engineering and unnecessary complexity rather than correctness, security, or compatibility risk; useful as a third-party candidate for skill-recommender.
